semantic.label: odd opcode loop with limit 1 returns 0
even opcode ignores masked loop handler
even program ignores odd loop body
even program ignores odd loop body
odd path: limit=1, loop body adds 0
n=0 again (mask drops bit 4 of n)
shift=0, x=1: buf shifts up by 1 each
seed=20: i8 wrap on high indices (-160 u32)
seed=0x8000: i16 wraps to negative in b[]
seed=40000: both b[] and c[] wrap
x=0, n=1: zero state stays zero
x=0x1FF, amount=32, n=8: rotation invariant for amount=32 since (lo<->hi) twice = identity but here 8 swaps = identity
max u64, n=8, p=0x100: telescope wraps
x=0: state=1, n=1: bit=1 shifted to MSB
max u64, n=16: clears top 16 bits
x=0: zero stays zero (no shift contribution)
x=0, n=1: max stays at val=0 (INT64_MIN beats nothing)
x=1, n=2: bit0=1 then bit1=0 -> 1 then 2
x=0x3F, n=64: 6 set bits low + 58 high zeros
max u64, n=64: 3*x+1 every iter wraps mod 2^64
max u64, n=16: every op=7 -> s ^= s>>5
x=0, n=1: val=0 even, no accumulate
x=0: crc init=1, n=1, single CRC step
max u64, n=16: wraps and opt2 wins many iters
x=0xFF, n=32: i=0 val=255 stays minimum
max u64, n=32: small via xor
x=1, n=2: bit0=1 rotl7, bit1=0 rotr11
x=1, n=2: i=0 odd, i=1 even
x=0, n=1, op=0: r0=0+~0=max; xor result = 0xCAFEBABE
x=0: src=0, mask=1, no bits set
x=0xF: src=0xF, mask=1, only bit 0 fits
max u64: src=max32 deposited at all 32 mask positions
x=0xCAFE: src=0xCAFE, mask=1 (high zero), bit 0 of src is 0
x=1: prefix-xor propagates 0x01 through all 8 bytes -> 0x0101010101010101
x=3: both bit 0 of evens and odds
x=0: target=0, all 7 lower bytes match
x=0: r stays 0 across all iters
x=2^63: high bit folds via lshr 47
x=0 n=1: hash of one zero byte
x=7 n=8: byte 7 ends up at byte position 7 (high) of r
x=7 n=8: nibble 7 ends up at high pos -> 0x70000000
x=0 n=1: hash of one zero byte + fold
x=0: r stays 0 across all iters
x=0 n=1: zigzag(0)=0, then s steps but loop ends
all 0xFF: x<<3 has low bits clear, sub wraps
0xCAFEBABE: n=7 odd trip leaves r near s+offset
all 0xFF n=8: telescoping cancels to small constant
all 0xFF: n=8 chunks all 0b11 xor stack
all 0xFF: ashr fills 1s; 8 xor of 0xFF cancel to 0
x=2^63 n=1: byte0=0 single iter (high bit only)
0xCAFEBABE: n=7 mixed-sign bytes scaled by index
x=0: r stays 0 (NOT AND 0 = 0; xor i<<3 keeps in low bits but r=0&0)
all 0xFF: ~r is single-bit, AND with all-1 keeps it; eight xor i<<3 over [0..56] flips 8 bytes
all 0xFF: n=8 -> XOR of 0xFF*1..0xFF*8 cancels to 0 (sum of 1..8=36 even count)
x=7 n=8: only byte0=7 nonzero, then 0
x=8 n=1: 1*8=8 (no zero byte to wreck)
all 0xFF n=8: sum 1..8=36 (counter low bits all kept)
0xCAFEBABE: n=7 OR of high-byte BE=0xBE | counters fills low 8 bits
all 0xFF n=8: 0xFF placed at 0,3,6,9,...,21 bit positions then XORed
x=7 n=8: byte0=7 produces shl by 7 of 0=0
x=8 n=1: shl by 0=0; OR byte>>4=0
all 0xFF: shl by 7 each iter combined with OR 0xF
all 0xFF: shr by 7 each iter; final r=1 ^ 0xFF=0xFE wait actually 1
all 0xFF: ashr fills 1s -> stable -1 + 0xFF, several iters
x=7 n=8: byte0=7 ends up at byte 0 after 8 right-shifts
0xDEADBEEF: n=8 - all bytes traverse top->bottom; result equals input low 32 bits
all 0xFF: a|b stays ~0; b evolves via XOR-mul *7
all 0xFF n=8: cumulative shift 36 bits + 0xFF bytes
x=7 n=8: cumulative right shift 36 bits
all 0xFF n=8: cumulative shift collapses then XOR 0xFF stack
x=7 n=8: ashr collapses then bytes 0
all 0xFF: ashr fills 1s -> r=-1 each iter; final +0xFF*8 wraps via -1+sums
all 0xFF: cascades but ends at all-1s
0xDEADBEEF: n=2 first quad XOR + second quad (zeros)
x=3 n=4: chain over 4 zero-padded iters
0xCAFEBABE: n=1 dword high bit set, sext negative -> 2^64-magnitude
0x12345...EF0: n=1 lower dword high bit set
0xCAFEBABE: n=7 high bit smears down covering low 32
x=2^63 n=1: Gray code propagates one bit down
0xCAFEBABE: max run=7, n=7 shrinks all to 0
all 0xFF n=8: shrink from low end -> high 56-1 mask
all 0xFF n=8: clear low 8 set bits
x=7 n=8: byte0=7 ascend through factorial-weighted
x=1 n=2: bytes [1,0] - only first matches
all 0xFF n=8: all bytes match first
0xCAFEBABE: n=7, BE BA FE CA all > 0x80
x=0x7F n=8: byte0=0x7F clamps to 0x7F (in range), rest 0->0x40 -> 127+7*64=575
x=1 n=2: byte0=1 bit0=1; byte1=0 bit1=0
all 0xFF n=8: every byte's bit i is set
x=7 n=8: only byte0=7 then zeros decreasing
